questions.question: By the end of which century had the Abbasid Caliphate declined?
questions.explanation: The Abbasid Caliphate declined by the end of the 9th century and disintegrated into several states ruled by the Turks.

questions.question: Which two important Turkish states emerged after the decline of the Abbasid Caliphate?
questions.explanation: After the disintegration of the Abbasid Caliphate, two important states that emerged were Ghazni and Ghor.

questions.question: How many times did Mahmud of Ghazni invade India?
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India 17 times in a span of 25 years, from 1001 to 1025 CE.

questions.question: Over what period did Mahmud of Ghazni carry out his invasions of India?
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India 17 times over a period of 25 years from 1001 to 1025 CE.

questions.question: Who was the ruler of Punjab defeated by Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni defeated Jaipal, the ruler of Punjab. After his defeat, Jaipal burnt himself to death.

questions.question: What did Jaipal do after being defeated by Mahmud of Ghazni?
options: D. Formed an alliance with other rulers
questions.explanation: After his defeat at the hands of Mahmud of Ghazni, Jaipal burnt himself to death rather than live in disgrace.

questions.question: Who was Anandapal?
questions.explanation: Anandapal was Jaipal's son. He formed an alliance with other rulers to fight Mahmud of Ghazni but was defeated.

questions.question: Which famous temple did Mahmud of Ghazni attack in Kathiawar?
questions.explanation: The most famous of Mahmud's attacks on temple towns was the raid on the Somnath Temple in Kathiawar, where about 50,000 soldiers died defending it.

questions.question: How many soldiers died defending the Somnath Temple against Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.explanation: About 50,000 soldiers died defending the Somnath Temple when Mahmud of Ghazni attacked it. He carried away immense treasures.

questions.question: Which was the only Indian province that Mahmud of Ghazni annexed?
questions.explanation: Despite invading India 17 times, Punjab was the only Indian province that Mahmud of Ghazni actually annexed to his kingdom.

questions.question: Mahmud of Ghazni patronized Firdausi, who wrote which famous work?
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni was a patron of art and learning. He patronized Firdausi, who wrote the famous Shahnama.

questions.question: Al Beruni, patronized by Mahmud of Ghazni, was a famous:
questions.explanation: Al Beruni was a renowned historian and mathematician who was patronized by Mahmud of Ghazni.

questions.question: When did the First Battle of Tarain take place?
questions.explanation: The First Battle of Tarain took place in 1191, in which Prithviraj Chauhan defeated Muhammad Ghori.

questions.question: Who won the First Battle of Tarain (1191)?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Chauhan defeated Muhammad Ghori in the First Battle of Tarain in 1191.

questions.question: Who refused to help Prithviraj Chauhan against Muhammad Ghori in the Second Battle of Tarain?
questions.explanation: Raja Jaichandra of Kanauj refused to help Prithviraj Chauhan, which contributed to his defeat in the Second Battle of Tarain in 1192.

questions.question: The defeat of Prithviraj Chauhan in 1192 marked the:
options: B. End of Rajput rule and beginning of Muslim rule
C. Start of the Maratha Empire
D. End of the Delhi Sultanate
questions.correct_answer: B. End of Rajput rule and beginning of Muslim rule
questions.explanation: The defeat of Prithviraj Chauhan in the Second Battle of Tarain (1192) marked the end of Rajput rule. Muslim rulers then ruled until 1858.

questions.question: Who did Muhammad Ghori appoint as his viceroy in India?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ghori appointed Qutbuddin Aibak as his viceroy to look after his Indian territories.

questions.question: Muhammad bin Bakhtiar Khilji is known for destroying Buddhist monasteries and libraries in which region?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Bakhtiar Khilji destroyed Buddhist monasteries and libraries in Bihar and also overthrew the Sena dynasty of Bengal.

questions.question: The Delhi Sultanate lasted from:
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate lasted from 1206 to 1526 CE. The rulers were called sultans.

questions.question: Qutbuddin Aibak was given the title 'Lakhbaksh' because he:
options: B. Was generous and gave away lakhs in charity
questions.correct_answer: B. Was generous and gave away lakhs in charity
questions.explanation: Qutbuddin Aibak was called 'Lakhbaksh' (giver of lakhs) because of his generosity in distributing wealth.

questions.question: Who is considered the true founder of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Although Qutbuddin Aibak established the dynasty, Iltutmish is considered the true founder of the Delhi Sultanate because he consolidated and organized it.

questions.question: Which ruler of the Delhi Sultanate was the first to mint gold and silver coins?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish was the first sultan of Delhi to mint gold and silver coins. He also completed the Qutb Minar.

questions.question: What was unique about Razia Sultan's rule?
options: A. She was the first female ruler in the world
B. She was Iltutmish's daughter who sat in open durbar and dressed like a man
D. She built the Qutb Minar
questions.correct_answer: B. She was Iltutmish's daughter who sat in open durbar and dressed like a man
questions.explanation: Razia Sultan, daughter of Iltutmish, was brave, intelligent, and just. She sat in open durbar and dressed like a man. She ruled from 1236 to 1239.

questions.question: Ghiyasuddin Balban believed in which concept of kingship?
questions.explanation: Ghiyasuddin Balban believed in the theory of divine kingship. He never smiled and made the monarchy absolute in authority.

questions.question: Which Rajput clan ruled Delhi and Ajmer?
questions.explanation: The five Rajput kingdoms included the Chauhans of Delhi and Ajmer, the Rathors of Kanauj, the Solankis of Gujarat, the Paramaras of Malwa, and the Chandellas of Bundelkhand.
